help.role: Choose Source to capture and send audio, or Sink to receive and play a stream.
help.inputDevice: Selects the microphone, monitor, loopback, or virtual device used to capture audio.
help.loopback: On Windows, captures the selected output device so system audio can be streamed directly.
help.transport: TCP maximizes compatibility; Native UDP adds discovery, jitter handling, and optional encryption.
help.mode: Client connects to a remote receiver; Server listens locally for incoming TCP connections.
help.psk: Encrypts Native UDP audio with a shared secret. Both endpoints must use the exact same key.
help.targetAddress: Sets the remote host and port that will receive audio when TCP Client mode starts.
help.port: Sets the local listening or Native UDP port. The same port must be reachable on the network.
help.allowlist: Limits TCP Server access to listed IP addresses or CIDR networks. Leave empty to allow all.
help.outputDevice: Selects the speakers or virtual output where received audio will be played.
help.sourceAddress: Sets the Native UDP source address to receive, using host:port format.
help.discoveredSources: Chooses a source found automatically on the local network and fills its address.

audio.title: Audio
audio.sampleRateHint: Sent to the receiver — must match e.g. Snapserver sampleformat
audio.adaptiveBufferDesc: Automatically adjust buffer size based on network jitter
audio.loopbackDesc: Capture system audio directly without virtual cables
audio.ultraLowWarning: Ultra-low latency + loopback may cause stuttering

help.inputDevice: Selects the microphone, monitor, loopback, or virtual device used to capture audio.
help.sampleRate: Sets the stereo s16le wire rate. It must match the format expected by the receiver.
help.bufferSize: Controls the capture callback size in frames. Smaller values reduce latency; larger values resist glitches.
help.format: PCM sends raw audio; WAV adds a stream header for browsers and media players in TCP Server mode.
help.loopback: On Windows, captures the selected output device so system audio can be streamed directly.
help.latencyProfile: Applies coordinated buffering presets. Use Balanced normally, Robust for unstable networks, or Custom for manual control.
help.ringBufferDuration: Sets how much captured audio the ring can retain. More time tolerates stalls but uses more memory.
help.chunkSize: Sets samples handled per network write. Smaller chunks can reduce latency but increase processing overhead.
help.adaptiveBuffer: Automatically adjusts buffering within the configured limits as network jitter changes.
help.minBuffer: Sets the lowest buffer duration the adaptive controller may use when the network is stable.
help.maxBuffer: Sets the highest buffer duration the adaptive controller may use during network instability.

help.profile: Loads a saved group of connection, audio, and automation settings for quick reuse.
help.autoStart: Launches TCP Streamer automatically when your operating-system session starts.
help.autoStream: Starts streaming after the application loads using the currently selected profile.
help.autoReconnect: Retries a lost TCP Client connection automatically until the receiver is available again.
help.language: Changes all interface labels and contextual help between English and Spanish.
